changes_made: Renamed k8s_etcd_encryption_enabled to k8s_etcd_encryption_at_rest_enabled for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to the cryptographic protection of information at rest. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a slight adjustment was made to improve clarity and consistency with the control's focus on 'at rest' encryption.
naming_improvements: Ensure that function names explicitly mention 'at_rest' when referring to encryption at rest to avoid ambiguity.
title: Protection Of Information At Rest - Protection of information at rest | cryptographic protection implement cryptogra
step1_reasoning: The control SC-28(1) focuses on the cryptographic protection of information at rest. In Kubernetes, this is primarily applicable to the encryption of Secrets and the etcd database, which stores cluster state and sensitive data. Enabling encryption for Secrets ensures that sensitive information is protected at rest. Similarly, enabling encryption for etcd, along with TLS for data in transit, aligns with the control's requirements for cryptographic protection.
